claims.public_wording: HeliX compresses Transformer KV cache on the verified GPU suite while preserving generated-token match against the baseline.

headline_metrics.reason: Delivered the strongest real compression against native bf16 across all primary GPU models while keeping generated match true.

claims.caveat: This claim is limited to the measured Qwen/SmolLM Transformer suite and does not cover hybrid recurrent state.

claims.public_wording: On local Zamba2, the strongest hybrid runtime result is architecture-aware: KV compression where KV exists plus recurrent-state compression where Mamba dominates.
claims.caveat: This is a CPU-local Zamba2-1.2B result; GPU hybrid validation remains a next milestone.

claims.public_wording: HeliX can serialize and restore a complete hybrid Zamba2 session with bit-perfect SHA-256 snapshot integrity.
claims.caveat: Hash matching proves serialized/restored snapshot integrity; by itself it does not prove semantic understanding after restore.

claims.public_wording: Under the local restore-equivalence probe, deterministic continuation from the restored hybrid state matched the pre-restore continuation.
claims.caveat: This is a short deterministic probe, not a broad semantic evaluation or long-context benchmark.

claims.public_wording: On an adversarial context-switch prompt, HeliX promoted blocks to higher precision and kept logits finite instead of forcing INT4 at all costs.
claims.caveat: The runtime-cache ratio dropped during this stress case, which is expected graceful degradation rather than maximum compression.

claims.public_wording: helix-watch provides a Rust terminal playback surface for mission telemetry and compact receipts.
claims.caveat: The TUI is playback-only in v1; it does not run inference or stream live sockets.

claims.public_wording: The staged Long-Context Coder mission showed compression and a marginal speedup, but did not recover hidden identifiers.
claims.caveat: Identifier recall was 0/2, so this should not be used as a quality claim yet.

claims.public_wording: The local prompt suite suggests code/agentic prompts may be a useful evaluation lane for hybrid memory compression.
claims.caveat: The current evidence is small and local; it is not a general agent benchmark.

claims.public_wording: HXQ Zamba2 remains a separate weight-compression lane under reproduction, not a verified HeliX runtime claim.
claims.caveat: Local runs have shown loader/runtime or non-finite-logit issues; HXQ is not included in public quality claims yet.

claims.public_wording: Zamba2-2.7B is intentionally out of local scope for this 12GB RAM laptop profile.
claims.caveat: This is a hardware budget boundary, not an architectural limitation.

claims.public_wording: HeliX now reports cold-prefill TTFT versus warm restored-session TTFT for local agent-memory evidence.
claims.caveat: This is a local CPU evidence sprint on small cached models; it is not a larger-model TTFT claim like Shkolnikov's Apple Silicon suite.

claims.public_wording: HeliX now reports how many measured or projected agent sessions fit inside a fixed memory budget.
claims.caveat: Rows marked projection=true are linear projections from the measured context length, not direct 4K-context runs.

claims.public_wording: HeliX has a local Session OS catalog that indexes model/agent sessions with audit status, token hashes and parent-chain metadata.
claims.caveat: This is the local Session OS control plane; it does not imply semantic quality by itself.

claims.public_wording: HeliX can resolve exact-prefix Transformer sessions and compute only new follow-up tokens in the local prefix-reuse path.
claims.caveat: Prefix reuse v0 is Transformer-only and exact-prefix. The compressed claim is top-1 stable with finite logits; the native-dense diagnostic is not bit-exact in the current local artifact.

claims.public_wording: HeliX exposes a local OpenAI-shaped chat completions response with HeliX session lifecycle metadata.
claims.caveat: The first endpoint is non-streaming and local; broader client compatibility still needs external framework tests.

claims.public_wording: HeliX can reuse an exact saved prefix checkpoint for Zamba2 hybrid sessions by restoring Transformer KV plus Mamba recurrent state, then computing only the suffix.
claims.caveat: Hybrid prefix v0 restores only exact saved checkpoints; partial Mamba prefix slicing remains unsupported, and the local artifact proves top-1/finitude rather than bit-exact logits.

claims.public_wording: HeliX can represent shared base sessions plus per-agent deltas as a verified parent-chain, a first step toward Git-like LLM session branching.
claims.caveat: Branching v1 is metadata plus avoided-rewrite estimate; physical token-slice storage is not implemented yet.

claims.public_wording: A standard OpenAI-compatible client can call HeliX's local chat completions surface and receive session lifecycle metadata.
claims.caveat: The showcase validates client plumbing; real LangChain/CrewAI workflows and streaming remain future work.

claims.public_wording: HeliX now has a local agent-recall catalog that stores observations and distilled memories beside verified session state, without adding a vector database or external memory server.
claims.caveat: This proves dependency-light recall plumbing and bounded context injection; it does not claim vector-search quality comparable to AgentMemory's embedding path.

claims.public_wording: HeliX agents can now automatically observe tool calls into hmem, inject bounded memory context at session start, and query memory plus knowledge through one hybrid search surface.
claims.caveat: This is local wiring evidence with heuristic/model-optional observation compression; it does not claim embedding-grade retrieval or external framework orchestration quality.

claims.public_wording: HeliX can run a Blueprint workload that coordinates agents, private .hlx state, shared hmem and a scheduler to produce a quality-first Meta Microsite artifact.
claims.caveat: Fallback or mixed mode proves orchestration, hmem, .hlx private state and renderer quality; it does not prove model generation quality.

claims.public_wording: HeliX can address .hlx cache payloads by layer metadata and load a requested layer slice without loading unrelated logical cache arrays.
claims.caveat: Layer-slice v1 returns CPU arrays and does not use raw pointer injection into PyTorch tensors.

claims.public_wording: HeliX has a dependency-free AirLLM-style bridge seam that activates a layer, injects only that layer's cache slice, runs a mock layer, and unloads it.
claims.caveat: This is not a real AirLLM giant-model run and no AirLLM fork is vendored into the main repo yet.

claims.public_wording: HeliX MemoryCatalog can preserve local concurrent agent observations and memories under the SQLite WAL pattern.
claims.caveat: This is a local single-process/threaded stress test with one SQLite connection per worker; it is not a distributed database claim.

claims.public_wording: Under a tight recall budget, HeliX keeps high-importance memories ahead of low-priority scratch memories.
claims.caveat: This validates deterministic lexical and priority-based selection; it does not claim embedding-quality semantic retrieval.

claims.public_wording: HeliX full verification blocks a corrupted .hlx layer slice before the injector returns it for layer execution.
claims.caveat: The fast receipt-only path is intentionally not an integrity claim; full verification or completed deferred audit is required for cryptographic safety claims.

claims.public_wording: HeliX now has a local Rust/Python layer-slice soak to watch for obvious hot-path RSS drift over repeated save/load cycles.
claims.caveat: A short soak is a regression guard, not a formal proof that long-running FFI memory management is leak-free.

claims.public_wording: A real AirLLM end-to-end smoke is available as an optional gate, but it is not a verified HeliX claim unless AirLLM and a local compatible model are already present.
claims.caveat: The default local run may skip cleanly because AirLLM is not installed or no local model path was provided.
